channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: In part of swing state Michigan, neighbors skip presidential signs in a heated campaign season
item.description: BLOOMFIELD TOWNSHIP, Mich. (AP) — The lawn of Nick Hannawa's suburban Detroit home is lined with political signs backing candidates for prosecutor, supervisor and local trustee. But Hannawa isn't promoting his presidential pick. He says he doesn't want the headaches in an polarized election year.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS and MIKE HOUSEHOLDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans and Democrats are both preparing for long legal battles over the 2024 election results
item.description: Both Republicans and Democrats are preparing for a potentially lengthy battle in the courts over the results of the 2024 election.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer, Colleen Long And Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Harris says Trump is 'cruel' as she spotlights abortion restrictions in Georgia during early voting
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris said Saturday that Republican former President Donald Trump was “cruel” for how he talked about the grieving family of a Georgia mother who died after waiting 20 hours for a hospital to treat her complications from an abortion pill, as she put combating re
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Child labor violations on the rise, problem could get worse: report
item.description: Between 2011 and 2020, the rate of injuries among workers under 18 nearly doubled, a recent report says.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: North Carolina sees record turnout for early voting after hurricane
item.description: "The conditions improve each and every day," the executive director of North Carolina's State Board of Elections said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kooiman, NewsNation

item.title: Judges punishing Jan. 6 rioters say they fear more political violence as Election Day nears
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Over the past four years, judges at Washington’s federal courthouse have punished hundreds of rioters who stormed the U.S. Capitol in an unprecedented assault on the nation's democracy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP-leaning polls trigger questions about accuracy
item.description: The prevalence of Republican-leaning polls in recent weeks is raising questions about their effect on the overall polling average as the presidential race between Vice President Harris and former President Trump tightens even further.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fetterman issues warning to Democrats after Elon stumps for Trump in Pennsylvania
item.description: Pennsylvania Senator John Fetterman told the New York Post in an interview that Elon Musk's endorsement of former President Trump is "a concern" in the Keystone State.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Massachusetts, Nevada, New Mexico launch early in-person voting
item.description: Early voting is underway in Massachusetts, Nevada and New Mexico, with just 17 days to go until the general election on Nov. 5. Find out how to vote in this article.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump teases he will 'do everything' while working behind McDonald's counter in crucial swing state
item.description: Former President Trump is making an unusual campaign stop this weekend in battleground Pennsylvania. He will be behind the counter, working the fry cooker at a McDonald's.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Trump and Harris are campaigning for votes in pivotal Michigan
item.description: With the Nov. 5 election fast approaching, Democrat Kamala Harris and Republican Donald Trump are setting their sights on the key battleground state of Michigan on Friday. The vice president began her day in Grand Rapids before holding events in Lansing and Oakland County, northwest of Detroit.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Poll finds majority of Americans say they're worse off than four years ago, similar to 1992 election
item.description: A Gallup poll suggested that the majority of Americans believe they are worse off than they were four years ago, citing economic strain as a top issue.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Montana GOP candidate who could flip control of Senate nagged by claims he lied about bullet wound
item.description: A former Park Service ranger says U.S. Senate hopeful Tim Sheehy of Montana has been lying about a bullet wound that the candidate said came from fighting in Afghanistan.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Home stretch: Harris, Trump hold dueling events, rallies in crucial battleground state
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris and former President Trump held dueling campaign events in battleground Michigan with less than three weeks to go until Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge unseals heavily redacted trove of evidence in Trump's 2020 election interference case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The judge overseeing Donald Trump's 2020 election interference case made public Friday a heavily redacted trove of documents that provide a small glimpse into the evidence prosecutors will present if the case ever goes to trial.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics: Crunch time for Harris
item.description: Get the latest updates from the 2024 campaign trail, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kentucky Tiktok suit says company considered using KY creators to sway Sen. Mitch McConnell
item.description: The suit says videos by Kentucky small businesses were identified as possibly of interest to the senator, who has expressed concern about the app's effect on kids
item.dc:creator: Sylvia Goodman, MPR News

item.title: Trump compares imprisoned Jan. 6 rioters to Japanese Americans during WWII
item.description: Nearly 900 people have pleaded guilty to a variety of federal charges in relation to the events of Jan. 6.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas Republicans demand answers about Venezuelan gang members
item.description: White House spokesperson Angelo Fernández Hernández has called reports Biden is "secretly flying" people into the U.S. “categorically false.
item.dc:creator: Katie Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Election 2024: How the 'New York Seven' could decide the House
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York voters have much to consider on Election Day. In addition to voting for the next president of the United States, New Yorkers will be asked to decide on a proposed amendment to the state Constitution, a U.S. Senate seat, and House representatives.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Bret Baier says he made 'a mistake' during Harris interview
item.description: Fox News anchor Bret Baier said former President Donald Trump's comments about "the enemy within" was mistakenly absent from a clip shown.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Barbed wire, high fences: some election spots in Arizona close, but others step up
item.description: Some election locations in Arizona are shutting their doors in the face of security threats — but others are expanding their footprint.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: AI-generated images have become a new form of propaganda this election season
item.description: This election cycle, AI-generated images have proliferated on social media platforms after politically charged news events. They often spread partisan narratives rather than facts.
item.dc:creator: Huo Jingnan, MPR News

item.title: As campaign nears finish, Walz adds dashes of digs to his portions of joy
item.description: The Democratic vice presidential candidate entered the race emphasizing positivity in politics. In the final stretch to Election Day, he’s trying to thread the needle between needling Donald Trump and JD Vance while still keeping an optimistic message.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump would be the oldest person to become president. He’s not sharing health details
item.description: If he wins next month’s election, Donald Trump would be the oldest person in U.S. history elected president. Yet the 78-year-old Republican nominee is refusing to disclose new details about his physical or mental well-being, breaking decades of precedent.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Nebraska court allows voter registrations from people with felony convictions
item.description: Nebraskans with felony convictions may register to vote after the state's highest court ruled Wednesday that a top election official had no authority to deem a law restoring those rights unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Harris is pressed on the concerns of Black voters in Charlamagne tha God town hall
item.description: Harris’ town hall with the influential radio host came at a crucial moment in the campaign, as polls show her struggling to cement the level of support she may need from Black men to win the White House.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram and Jason Breslow, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota city says Trump campaign still owes more than $200,000 for July rally
item.description: Local officials say former President Donald Trump’s campaign still owes a central Minnesota city nearly $209,000 for services related to a July campaign rally. The Minnesota Star Tribune reports that the bill is for services beyond what St. Cloud normally provides.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Donald Trump addresses Economic Club of Chicago
item.description: CHICAGO — Former President Donald Trump is back in Chicago Tuesday. Trump addressed the Economic Club of Chicago at Fairmont Chicago, located at 200 North Columbus Drive, ahead of the Nov. 5 election.
item.dc:creator: Christine Flores, WGN 9
